sources.name: Silver Institute – World Silver Survey

metrics: Mine production (Moz and tonnes)
Recycling supply by source (industrial, jewelry, silverware, photography, coin) (Moz/tonnes)
Net hedging supply/demand (Moz)
Net official sector sales (Moz)
Total supply (Moz)
Industrial demand – total (Moz)
Industrial demand – by sector: electrical & electronics, photovoltaics (PV), brazing alloys & solders, other industrial, photography (Moz)
Jewelry fabrication (Moz)
Silverware fabrication (Moz)
Coin & net bar demand (Moz)
Market balance (surplus/deficit in Moz)
Net investment in ETPs and ETP holdings (Moz)
Silver price – nominal and real, multiple currencies; LBMA price history; gold:silver ratio
data_available.granularity: Global totals by year; appendices provide tonnage and ounce data by year over multi‑year history

metrics: Mine production by region and by country (Top 20 producing countries; Top 20/30 producing companies and mines) (Moz/tonnes)
Mine production by primary metal source (primary silver, gold, copper, lead/zinc) (regional, annual)
Industrial demand by region and sector (Europe, North America, Latin America, East Asia, South Asia, Middle East, CIS, Others) (Moz/tonnes)
Jewelry and silverware demand by region and selected countries (Moz/tonnes)
Physical investment (bars, coins) by region and key countries (e.g., US, India, Europe, East Asia) (Moz/tonnes)
Recycling by region and sector (Moz/tonnes)
data_available.granularity: Regional and country‑level, annual; some tables cover ~2016–2025+ history and forecasts (e.g., 2025, 2026F)

metrics: Physical investment by region and country (Moz)
Coin and medals fabrication (Moz) by region
Exchange‑traded product (ETP) holdings by product and custodian (monthly, Moz)
Annual turnover on major commodity exchanges (CME, SHFE, MCX, SGE T+D) and LBMA spot/forwards (Moz)
CME activity & inventories – open interest, turnover, inventory aggregates (appendix level)
LBMA silver trading volumes – monthly and yearly by category
London vault inventories and estimated ‘free float’
Global bullion trade flows – imports/exports for key hubs (Swiss, UK, Hong Kong, India, etc.)
data_available.granularity: Exchange / product / region level; mix of monthly and annual series depending on table

metrics: Primary silver mine cash costs, total cash cost (TCC), all‑in sustaining cost (AISC) by region
Reserve and resource data (global and by major asset/region)
Hedge book evolution – delta‑adjusted positions by year (forwards vs options)
Major streaming/royalty volumes and select deal metrics
Top 30 silver mines – production, operator, country

sources.update_frequency: Annual (World Silver Survey published once per year covering previous calendar year; some supplemental Interim Reviews also annual).
sources.access_type: World Silver Survey PDFs and many related publications are freely downloadable from The Silver Institute website; data is compiled by specialist consultancy (Metals Focus) from hallmarking series, trade statistics, company reports, and other public-domain and proprietary information, and is widely cited by industry and institutional research.

sources.name: CME Group – COMEX silver inventory and futures

metrics: Depository name
Prev total inventory (oz)
Received (oz) – daily change
Withdrawn (oz) – daily change
Net change (oz)
Adjustment (oz)
Total today (oz)
Registered stocks by depository and total (oz)
Eligible stocks by depository and total (oz)
Combined total (registered + eligible) (oz)
data_available.granularity: Per approved depository plus exchange‑wide totals, reported in fine ounces on a daily warehouse stocks XLS.

data_available.category: Futures and options market data (high level, via CME public pages)
metrics: Front and deferred contract prices (futures)
Daily settlement prices
Volume (contracts)
Open interest (contracts)
Basic contract specs (contract size, tick, delivery terms, eligible vs registered definitions via rulebooks)
data_available.granularity: By individual futures and options contract month; daily end‑of‑day on public site; tick‑by‑tick and full depth via paid market data feeds.

sources.update_frequency: Warehouse stocks report: daily at end of trading day; futures and options quotes: intraday (real‑time) with official settlements once per trading day.
sources.access_type: Headline futures/warehouse data is freely accessible on CME’s website (including daily Silver_stocks.xls and contract pages). Full tick‑level data and low‑latency feeds require paid CME/redistributor market data subscriptions through brokers or data vendors.

sources.name: LBMA – London vault holdings (London Vault Data)

metrics: Total Loco London silver held in LBMA‑recognized vaults (tonnes and equivalent troy ounces)
Total Loco London gold holdings (tonnes) – useful for cross‑metal context
Occasional breakdowns including/excluding Bank of England holdings (when published)
Contextual conversions (e.g., approximate number of good‑delivery bars implied by total holdings in some LBMA commentary)
data_available.granularity: System‑wide aggregate (London vaulting system) for silver and gold; no per‑vault or per‑owner detail in public series.

sources.update_frequency: Typically monthly or quarterly public releases; commentary from LBMA and secondary analytics sources indicate data is published at least quarterly, with many recent references to monthly tonnage changes.
sources.access_type: Aggregate vault data is published free on LBMA’s website and republished by various data aggregators (e.g., MetalCharts, GoldSilver.ai). Underlying position‑level data remains confidential; only systemwide aggregates are public.

sources.name: Bloomberg – silver lease rates and ETF holdings (via Bloomberg Terminal & LBMA Trade Data)

data_available.category: LBMA silver trade and lease‑related volumes (via LBMA Trade Data on Bloomberg)
metrics: Silver loan/lease/deposit volume by tenor (e.g., 1 week, 2 weeks, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, 9 months, 12 months, 1 year+)
Silver swap/forward volume by tenor (same tenor buckets)
Silver option volume by tenor
Sub‑metrics for each tenor: total volume and spot volume
Silver spot volume (daily and weekly)
Silver spot total volume (aggregated)
data_available.granularity: Tenor‑by‑tenor volumes for loans/leases, deposits, swaps/forwards, and options; daily and weekly datasets.

data_available.category: Silver lease rates (implied borrowing costs in London market)
metrics: Implied silver lease rates by tenor (e.g., 1‑month, 3‑month, 6‑month, 1‑year) – annualized percentage
Time series of lease rates used in news and analytics (spot articles reference 1‑month and 1‑year implied lease rates surging to 5–6%+)
Associated reference series: LIBOR/benchmark funding rates and forward/spot spreads used in lease‑rate construction (internal to Bloomberg calculations)
data_available.granularity: Tenor‑specific rates with at least daily time series; intraday snapshots available in terminal functions.

data_available.category: Silver ETF and ETP holdings (Bloomberg funds/ETP data)
metrics: Holdings of physically‑backed silver ETFs and ETPs by fund (ounces/tonnes and market value)
Daily changes in holdings and shares outstanding
Fund flows and AUM for silver‑linked products (including leveraged/inverse ETPs where relevant)
Aggregated global ETF silver holdings (sum across products), used in Bloomberg commentary and screens
data_available.granularity: Fund‑level daily holdings and flows; global aggregates available via custom screens; historical time series.

sources.update_frequency: LBMA trade data on Bloomberg: daily for full fee‑liable dataset (LBMA6), with delayed weekly data available free (LBMA7). Silver lease rates and ETF holdings: updated throughout the trading day with official fund holdings typically updated daily or when issuers publish changes.
sources.access_type: Access is primarily via the Bloomberg Terminal and B‑PIPE feeds. Daily LBMA trade data (LBMA6 <GO>) is fee‑liable; delayed weekly LBMA data (LBMA7 <GO>) is free to terminal users. Overall Bloomberg access requires an institutional‑grade subscription (typical cost around \$24,000–\$32,000 per terminal per year in recent pricing).

sources.name: ICE – U.S. Dollar Index (DXY)

metrics: Constituent currencies and fixed weights:
Exact calculation formula for USDX as a weighted geometric mean of FX rates (EURUSD, USDJPY, GBPUSD, USDCAD, USDSEK, USDCHF)
Base value: 100.000 at inception (March 1973)
data_available.granularity: Index‑level only; no per‑constituent index series beyond the underlying FX rates themselves.

metrics: Historical yearly closes (DXY close by year)
Historical extremes (e.g., all‑time high 164.720 in Feb 1985; major lows like 70.698 in March 2008)
Ongoing real‑time level (index points)
Intraday high/low, previous close, 52‑week high/low on most data platforms
data_available.granularity: At least daily close series; real‑time tick level via ICE and major market data vendors.

sources.update_frequency: USDX/DXY is recalculated continuously whenever FX markets are open. Effective coverage from Sunday evening (New York time) through late Friday afternoon with near 24‑hour updates.
sources.access_type: ICE is the official administrator and publishes index levels via its data services; real‑time DXY is broadly redistributed (CNBC, Bloomberg, Yahoo Finance, trading platforms). Basic delayed/real‑time quotes and daily history are freely viewable on many finance sites; professional low‑latency and full historical feeds require paid ICE or vendor data subscriptions.
